title: SMSociety 2026 Conference Programme – #SMSociety 2026
snippet: Event announcement for the International Conference on Social Media & Society at the University of Glasgow. Prof. Fabio Giglietto is featured as a roundtable panelist alongside other global experts to discuss platform transparency and the limitations of utilizing the Meta Content Library (MCL) for independent academic research.
description: Prof. Fabio Giglietto is featured as a roundtable panelist at the SMSociety 2026 conference to discuss platform transparency and the limitations of the Meta Content Library for academic research.

title: Algoritmi e democrazia, il caso Meta secondo lo studio di UniUrb
snippet: Press coverage highlighting the institutional fallout of Prof. Fabio Giglietto's research. Following a feature on the Rai3 TV program 'Report' on April 12, 2026, the findings prompted an inquiry by the European Parliament and led Italy's communications regulatory authority (AGCOM) to summon Prof. Giglietto for a hearing on platform transparency and algorithmic impact on democratic processes.
description: Press coverage detailing the institutional impact of Prof. Fabio Giglietto's research on Meta's algorithms, which led to an inquiry by the European Parliament and a hearing with Italy's AGCOM.

title: Facebook, troppa visibilità a estremisti e complottisti: ecco come impatta Meta sulle elezioni
snippet: Media coverage of the research conducted by Prof. Fabio Giglietto on Meta's algorithmic distribution of political content. The article details how the algorithm's suppression mechanism disproportionately harms democratic institutional representatives while extremist and conspiracy accounts compensated by ramping up post frequencies.
description: An article covering Professor Fabio Giglietto's research analyzing over 2.5 million Facebook posts, focusing on how political extremist pages bypassed Meta's filters to gain visibility during elections.

title: Facebook premia gli estremisti: la ricerca dell'Università di Urbino - Lumsanews
Meno contenuti politici nei feed, la stretta di Meta è stata meno efficace contro gli estremisti - Il Sole 24 ORE
description: News article from Il Sole 24 ORE

title: Secondo uno studio dell'università di Urbino l'algoritmo di Facebook ora punisce la politica ufficiale e premia gli estremisti antieuropei. Pd e FdI verso una legge bipartisan sulle piattaforme
snippet: This news article reports on a study led by Prof. Fabio Giglietto from the University of Urbino [3.4.1]. The study analyzed over 2.5 million Facebook posts between 2021 and 2025, revealing that Meta's content reduction policies caused a dramatic decline in reach for mainstream elected officials while leaving extremist and fringe accounts mostly unaffected. The coverage highlights the political reactions in Italy, with parties proposing bipartisan regulatory legislation.
description: An Italian news article reporting on a study led by Prof. Fabio Giglietto from the University of Urbino, analyzing the impact of Facebook's algorithm on the reach of mainstream versus extremist political accounts.
